ogTitle: Turlinjah vs Turners Flat: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Turlinjah and Turners Flat.
heading: Turlinjah vs Turners Flat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Turlinjah, New South Wales and Turners Flat, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Turlinjah has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Turners Flat (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Turlinjah or Turners Flat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Turlinjah has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Turners Flat scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Turlinjah scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Turners Flat scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Turlinjah or Turners Flat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Turlinjah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
